skill.name: meeting-insights-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes local meeting transcripts to identify communication patterns, speaking behavior, facilitation habits, and actionable coaching opportunities.
skill.summary: Finds communication patterns and coaching insights in meeting transcripts.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but the cited lines are Markdown examples and ordinary prose rather than executable code. The real residual risk is low: the skill instructs the agent to scan local folders and analyze sensitive meeting transcripts, so users should control file scope and privacy.

low_findings.title: Local Transcript Privacy Scope
low_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to scan a folder for transcript files and analyze meeting content. This is expected behavior, but meeting transcripts can contain sensitive personal, business, or customer information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly ask the agent to scan local transcript files and also warn users to keep sensitive meeting data local. This confirms a privacy-relevant filesystem access concern without evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Malware Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are Markdown code fences and sample analysis templates, not executable commands. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits occur in descriptive text and common analysis wording, with no code implementing those behaviors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited ranges shows prose, fenced prompt examples, and sample report formats. No executable script, shell invocation, cryptographic operation, network scan, or prompt injection attempt is present.

content.value_statement: Meeting transcripts often hide patterns that are hard to see during live conversations. This skill turns local transcripts into clear feedback on speaking habits, facilitation style, listening, and growth areas.
actual_capabilities: Reviews local meeting transcript files in common text-based formats.
Identifies patterns such as conflict avoidance, interruptions, filler words, and active listening.
Calculates speaking ratios, question patterns, and turn-taking behavior when transcripts support it.
Provides timestamped examples and suggested alternative phrasing.
Compares communication trends across multiple meetings or time periods.
Summarizes strengths, growth areas, and practical next steps.
limitations: Requires transcript text with enough speaker labels to support reliable analysis.
Does not transcribe audio or video files by itself.
Insights are coaching feedback, not clinical or legal advice.
Benchmarks depend on the available meeting set and may not represent broader norms.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Leadership Review
use_cases.description: Analyze recent team meetings to find concrete examples of facilitation strengths, listening habits, and growth areas.

use_cases.title: Improve Sales or Customer Calls
use_cases.description: Review call transcripts to understand question quality, talk time, objection handling, and missed follow-up opportunities.

use_cases.title: Track Personal Communication Growth
use_cases.description: Compare transcripts across months or quarters to see whether interruptions, filler words, and direct feedback patterns improve.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Meeting Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis for one folder of transcripts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the transcripts in this folder. Summarize my main communication patterns, strengths, and three practical improvement steps.

prompt_templates.title: Conflict Avoidance Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of directness and difficult conversations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these meetings for moments when I avoided conflict or softened direct feedback. Include examples, likely impact, and better wording.

prompt_templates.title: Facilitation Style Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate analysis for recurring team meetings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my facilitation style across these team meetings. Cover speaking ratio, inclusion of quieter people, decision clarity, and follow-up quality.

prompt_templates.title: Quarterly Communication Trend
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the Q1 and Q2 transcript folders. Show changes in listening, interruptions, questions, filler words, and direct feedback, with evidence.

output_examples.input: A folder of weekly team meeting transcripts with speaker labels and timestamps.
output_examples.output: A concise communication report with key patterns, timestamped examples, speaking statistics, strengths, growth areas, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Two transcript folders from different quarters.
output_examples.output: A trend comparison showing changes in interruption frequency, clarifying questions, active listening, and remaining improvement areas.

output_examples.input: A set of customer call transcripts.
output_examples.output: A coaching summary covering talk time, question quality, objection handling, missed signals, and recommended follow-up behaviors.

best_practices: Use consistent file names with dates and meeting names.
Review one communication behavior at a time when you need depth.
Remove or restrict sensitive transcripts that are outside the analysis goal.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad conclusions from transcripts without speaker labels.
Do not treat the output as a complete performance evaluation by itself.
Do not include confidential meetings unless the agent should read them.

faq.question: What files does this skill work with?
faq.answer: It is designed for transcript files such as text, Markdown, VTT, SRT, and document exports that contain readable meeting text.

faq.question: Can it analyze audio recordings directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill expects transcripts. Use a transcription tool first, then provide the transcript files for analysis.

faq.question: Does it send meeting data to a third party?
faq.answer: No network transfer is instructed in the skill. Users should still keep transcript folders scoped to files they want analyzed.

faq.question: Can it compare progress over time?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare transcript sets from different periods when the files are organized clearly.

faq.question: How accurate are speaking ratios?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on transcript quality, timestamps, and speaker labels. Poor transcripts may produce approximate results.

faq.question: Is this suitable for performance reviews?
faq.answer: It can provide examples and patterns for reflection, but it should not be the only source for formal evaluation.
